opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** Running agents at the OS level fundamentally changes the permission model—you're trading process isolation for capability, but that creates real surface area for privilege escalation. If you're doing this, you need to think hard about agent state persistence across reboots; most teams over-index on the "always-on" hook and under-index on the recovery path when the agent fails or the OS patches out its APIs.
actions.reasoning: ** I have built multi-agent systems with OS-level tooling integration and can speak to the architectural tradeoffs of agent-OS coupling versus sandboxed execution.
